title: carex brevicollis
intro: Carex brevicollis, also known as the Short-tailed Sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. Traditionally, Carex brevicollis has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Carex brevicollis include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help to prevent infections. In herbal preparations, Carex brevicollis is often dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
